channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Google Gemini was a deadly ‘AI wife’ for this 36-year-old who resisted its call for a ‘mass casualty’ event before his death, lawsuit says
item.description: “Our models generally perform well in these types of challenging conversations ... but unfortunately AI models are not perfect,” Google said.

item.title: China Bets on Technology to Resist U.S. Pressure
item.description: China announced a 7 percent increase in military spending and a five-year plan to try to reduce its military and industry’s reliance on Western techno...

item.title: Asia faces an energy shock from the Iran war and a closed Strait of Hormuz, as governments halt exports and draw down stockpiles
item.description: Asian countries like China, Korea and Japan have tried to diversify their energy imports, yet they still depend on oil and gas from the Middle East.

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg, Adam Mosseri’s words used against them in never-before-seen videos airing in addiction trial
item.description: "I think that there’s over 2 billion people on Instagram, which means there are millions of teens on Instagram."

item.title: China Sets Its Lowest Economic Growth Target in Decades
item.description: The goal of between 4.5 percent and 5 percent, announced at a gathering of Communist Party leaders, was the lowest since 1991 and can offer clues abou...

item.title: Refugee Sentenced to 13 Years for Stabbing at Berlin Holocaust Memorial
item.description: A German court found that the attacker, now age 20, had traveled more than 100 miles to stab a Spanish tourist, who survived.

item.title: Does the Iran War Put America First?
item.description: How the antiwar right sees Trump’s foreign policy.

item.title: Time on his side: Michael Dell the real business icon as Icahn the activist recedes from view
item.description: In retrospect, the titanic battle between Dell and Icahn likely marked a turning point in activist investing.

item.title: Why women’s earnings plateau in their 30s while men’s just keep growing through their 40s: It’s not just motherhood
item.description: She enters the workforce at a disadvantage. By the time she turns 35, it's something structural and the numbers make it impossible to look away.

item.title: Refugee Sentenced to 13 Years for Stabbing at Berlin Holocaust Memorial
item.description: A German court found that the attacker, now age 20, had traveled more than 100 miles to stab a Spanish tourist, who survived.

item.title: China Bets on Technology to Resist U.S. Pressure
item.description: China announced a 7 percent increase in military spending and a five-year plan to try to reduce its military and industry’s reliance on Western techno...

item.title: As Iran Widens Retaliatory Strikes, Europe Accelerates Response for Gulf Allies
item.description: Defense Minister Guido Crosetto said that Italy would provide “air defense systems, anti-drone and antimissile systems” as Iran widened its retaliator...

item.title: Iceland Defeats Iceland: A U.K. Supermarket Ends a Trademark Dispute
item.description: Can a company trademark the name of a country? In the case of Iceland, the British grocery store, the answer is no.

item.title: On the Ground in Beirut
item.description: Our Beirut bureau chief, Christina Goldbaum, reports on the escalating conflict between Israel and the Iran-backed armed group Hezbollah, as Israel’s ...

item.title: Live Updates: Europe Ramps Up Response as Iranian Crisis Spills Beyond Mideast
item.description: Italy pledged air defense weapons to help Gulf nations defend against Iran’s retaliatory attacks. Iranian drones landed in Azerbaijan, a day after NAT...
